evals.prompt: Write an Ansible playbook to install and configure nginx on my web hosts.
evals.expected_output: A named playbook plus role using FQCN modules, idempotent tasks, a validated template, a notify/handler reload, explicit file modes, and role-prefixed variables in defaults.
expectations: Every task and play has a descriptive, capitalized name
Template task uses validate: and the file task sets mode explicitly
Service reload is done via notify + a named handler
Tunable values are role-prefixed variables in defaults/main.yml
Runs or offers to run ansible-lint / yamllint to verify

evals.prompt: Lint my Ansible repo and set up ansible-lint and yamllint for it.
evals.expected_output: Runs syntax-check, yamllint, and ansible-lint, returns a severity-ranked report, and scaffolds .yamllint, .ansible-lint, and pre-commit config if missing.
expectations: Reports findings ranked by severity (error / warning / nit)
Shows a diff before applying any fix

evals.prompt: Review this role for best practices.
evals.expected_output: A best-practices review covering idempotency, FQCN, validation, secrets/no_log, tags, handlers, and multi-OS guards, with concrete fixes.
expectations: Flags missing FQCN, missing task names, missing file mode
Checks secrets handling (Vault + no_log) and template validate:
Does not overwrite files silently; proposes diffs

evals.prompt: Convert this bash provisioning script into an idempotent Ansible role.
evals.expected_output: A role that maps each command to its native module, extracts literals into defaults variables, and wraps any module-less command with changed_when/creates.
expectations: Maps apt/cp/sed/systemctl/useradd to native modules rather than command:
Extracts hardcoded values into role-prefixed defaults variables
Wraps genuinely module-less commands with creates/changed_when
Produces standard role directory layout with meta/main.yml
